title: Dalea exserta
taxonomy: Dalea exserta is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is classified as a perennial shrub, with a growth habit that can reach up to 3 feet in height. Dalea exserta is a member of the tribe Fabae, within the subfamily Faboideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower morphology.
uses: Dalea exserta, a plant species, is said to have potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. intro: Dalea exserta, also known as the desert rose or silver prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
